context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Left

episode_text:
day=29; meal=bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce; setting=Watching TV with TV on Left; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals support a moderate reheating baseline. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE social savory meals across partner locations consistently support moderate reheating. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 1 min is the stronger general working baseline, with some left-working savory variation up to 2 min, BECAUSE cumulative working evidence mostly favors milder reheating while a few savory episodes show limited variation. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce and fruit, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed right-working dessert-like meal supports somewhat more reheating for that mixed sweet context than the user's usual savory-working pattern. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to microwave fruit intended to be served cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected moderate reheating for dessert-like items in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory meal indicates a stronger reheating preference there than in other contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory evidence supports a moderate reheating preference. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the new left-TV dessert-like meal shows the user may want stronger reheating for warm sweet foods in that context than for left-TV savory meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is savory food, THEN reheating is stronger than the user's personal baseline, with 1 min for a simpler sandwich-style savory meal and 3 min for a more substantial pizza-and-broccoli meal, BECAUSE right-TV evidence shows this context varies with meal type and the user may want hotter food for a heavier savory meal. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit or fruit with a cold topping, THEN no microwave BECAUSE personal fruit meals continue to support leaving cold items unheated.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals consistently show the user wants to preserve visibility toward the TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly to preserve visibility toward the social partner. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE observed left-social meals show the same side-matched visibility preference seen in other attended-target contexts. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-social meals show the user wants the robot to preserve the front view during face-to-face interaction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-working meals consistently show the user wants the robot to preserve the front view while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals continue to show the user aligns occlusion avoidance with the attended device location. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected across multiple front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals, now including a dessert-like meal, show a stable preference to preserve visibility toward the TV. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals indicate occlusion concerns do not generalize to that work setup. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated personal meals are consistent with the baseline that occlusion matters mainly when there is a salient person or screen to keep visible.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in that easy-to-serve, low-risk context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a moderate speed for a messier meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN slow BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user prefers more cautious motion for a hotter, more substantial meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations support a balanced speed preference. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed across observed working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN medium BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence supports a balanced speed preference there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN slow BECAUSE the new left-TV dessert-like meal shows the user becomes more cautious for a warm, messy sweet meal in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is savory food, THEN speed depends on meal complexity, with medium for simpler sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food and slow for a more substantial pizza-and-broccoli meal, BECAUSE right-TV evidence suggests the user becomes more cautious for heavier savory meals in that context. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated personal meals across sweet and savory foods continue to support a stable moderate baseline rather than either maximal speed or extra caution.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition during feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most solid savory foods, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly favored narrower bites for savory solids across personal, social, TV, and working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft dessert items such as brownies with sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in that meal, suggesting willingness to prioritize bite acquisition success for soft dessert foods. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies and banana pieces, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed episode supports the same dessert-related exception where the user accepts different acquisition geometry to improve pickup success for soft sweets. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the new left-TV dessert-like meal reinforces that soft sweet foods are a stable exception to the user's usual narrow-bite preference. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit with topping, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the personal strawberries-with-whipped-cream meal extends the pattern that the user accepts perpendicular skewering for soft sweet items where pickup reliability may matter more than minimizing bite width.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations support confirmation in social contexts, consistent with wanting to avoid awkward failed transfers. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is more complex fork-fed meals or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN yes BECAUSE several working meals support a preference for explicit bite-acquisition verification when attention is divided or bite acquisition may be messier. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a straightforward savory finger-food-style meal, THEN no BECAUSE the front-laptop buffalo chicken meal showed the user may skip confirmation in simpler working contexts to reduce interaction time. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that easier feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for a more complex, messier bite-acquisition context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user also wants explicit verification for substantial savory foods. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals support confirmation in that more complex feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the new left-TV dessert-like meal shows the same preference for explicit verification in a messy sweet context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is savory food, THEN confirmation depends on meal complexity, with no for a simpler sandwich-style meal and yes for a more substantial pizza-and-broccoli meal, BECAUSE right-TV evidence shows the user accepts extra verification when the meal is heavier and more manually controlled. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food with topping, hot savory fork-fed food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated personal meals continue to support explicit bite-acquisition verification even outside social or work settings. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit verification and faster flow AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simpler meal, THEN favor no when the overall interaction style is streamlined, with short autocontinue waits and low-mess food, and favor yes when the meal is messier, more delicate, more substantial, or otherwise manually controlled BECAUSE the cumulative evidence suggests the user trades confirmation for efficiency mainly in easier contexts.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and repeatedly selected the less intrusive transfer style in personal meals. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations support the less intrusive, user-controlled transfer style. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user continued to prefer the less intrusive transfer style across working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected outside-mouth transfer across those front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows that for a more substantial savory TV meal the user preferred reduced self-motion over the usual less-intrusive style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simpler sandwich-style savory meal, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE earlier right-TV evidence matched the user's broader preference for user-controlled, less intrusive delivery. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a more substantial savory meal such as pizza with vegetables, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal indicates the user may switch to reduced self-motion for heavier savory TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence indicates that even there the user generally prefers the less intrusive, user-controlled style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the new left-TV dessert-like meal shows that in TV contexts the user may also prefer reduced self-motion for warm, messy sweet foods. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated personal meals reinforce that the user generally prefers to lean in rather than have the robot enter the mouth when the context is calm.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance in observed personal meals. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations support a somewhat larger buffer in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance across working meals. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN near BECAUSE the user consistently selected a close stop distance in front-TV meals. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN near BECAUSE the observed outside-mouth right-TV meal reinforces a close stop preference when that transfer style is used. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN near BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence provides direct support for a close stop distance there. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is any observed state AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when inside-mouth transfer is chosen.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations show a stable preference for quieter signaling. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose LED during working meals, consistent with low-disruption visual cues during focused activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a preference for quiet visual cues when attention is directed toward media. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals, now including a dessert-like meal, align that context with the user's broader TV preference for quiet visual prompting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simpler savory meal, THEN LED BECAUSE earlier right-TV evidence showed the user can prefer quiet visual prompting there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a more substantial savory meal such as pizza with vegetables, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the newer right-TV meal shows the user may want a more salient cue in a slower, more manually controlled interaction. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user wants a more salient multimodal cue in quiet personal settings when there is no concern about disturbing others or media.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most previously observed meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less attractive, and many meals favored explicit button control. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals reinforce explicit manual initiation in social settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently selected manual initiation across working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN button BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user can return to explicit initiation in TV contexts when the meal is more substantial. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is savory food, THEN button BECAUSE both simpler and more substantial right-TV savory meals support explicit initiation in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip or a mixed sweet meal with sauce, THEN button BECAUSE both left-TV savory and dessert-like meals support explicit initiation there as well. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN button BECAUSE repeated personal meals are consistent with the user's strong overall preference for explicit manual initiation. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit control and automation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing meals, THEN tie-break by meal complexity and overall control style, favoring button when the user also chooses confirmation, slower speed, or other manual-control settings, and reserving autocontinue only for isolated simpler TV meals BECAUSE the user's broader pattern strongly favors explicit control.

IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most previously observed meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and often preferred direct manual control. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals reinforce explicit manual initiation in social settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button initiation across working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN button BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user preferred explicit drinking initiation in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is savory food, THEN button BECAUSE both simpler and more substantial right-TV savory meals support explicit drinking initiation. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip or a mixed sweet meal with sauce, THEN button BECAUSE left-TV evidence across both meal types supports explicit initiation there. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE repeated personal meals are consistent with the user's stable preference for manual drinking initiation. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit control and automation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing meals, THEN tie-break by overall interaction style, favoring button when the meal also uses confirmation and other manual-control settings, and reserving autocontinue for isolated simpler TV meals where the user otherwise accepted more automation BECAUSE the cumulative evidence favors manual control.

IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most previously observed meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and often preferred direct manual control. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals reinforce explicit manual initiation in social settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button initiation across working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip or a mixed sweet meal with sauce, THEN button BECAUSE left-TV evidence across both meal types supports explicit initiation there. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE repeated personal meals are consistent with the user's stable preference for manual wiping initiation. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit control and automation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing meals, THEN tie-break by overall interaction style, favoring button when the meal also uses confirmation and other manual-control settings, and reserving autocontinue for isolated simpler TV meals where the user otherwise accepted more automation BECAUSE the cumulative evidence favors manual control.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations show a stable preference for quieter completion cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected LED during working meals. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated social meals show the user prioritizes reducing prolonged obstruction when attention to a social partner is important. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-working meals show the user wants the robot to clear the front view between tasks. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals provide direct evidence that when the attended device is on the right, the user wants the robot to clear that view between tasks. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals across both savory and dessert-like foods show the user wants the robot to clear the left-side view between tasks. IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is savory TV meals, THEN yes is now the stronger tendency, BECAUSE both the earlier bite-sized pizza meal and the newer pizza-and-broccoli meal support clearing the right-side TV view between tasks. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals show the user does not want the extra time cost of retracting there. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals reinforce the baseline preference to avoid extra time cost when there is no external person or screen whose view must be preserved. IF dining context evidence conflicts between preserving visibility and reducing time AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is attended-target contexts, THEN tie-break by whether the user is prioritizing uninterrupted visibility and explicit control, favoring yes when the meal also uses confirmation, slower or medium speed, and other manual-control settings, and favoring no when the attended target has repeatedly not triggered occlusion concern, as in left-laptop work meals, or when no salient external target needs protection BECAUSE the observed TV, work, social, and personal meals fit that pattern.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE observed social meals across partner locations support a moderate, mess-conscious dipping preference. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left or right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but not maximal dipping across several side-working meals, supporting a moderate, mess-conscious working preference there. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE front-working savory evidence includes meals where the user explicitly declined available sauce, suggesting a stronger no-dip tendency when working directly in front, likely to reduce mess and distraction. IF food does not include a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed savory working meals reinforce that the user does not add dipping when it is unnecessary. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND food_condition is simple items where dipping is optional, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly declined dipping in one observed front-TV meal despite sauce availability, showing a conservative baseline. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a dessert meal with chocolate sauce, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping when the sauce was integral to the meal. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal with ranch dressing, THEN less BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals support moderate dipping rather than no dipping or heavy dipping. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN less BECAUSE the new left-TV dessert-like meal extends the same moderate, mess-conscious dipping preference to sweet sauce contexts there. IF food_condition is a meal without dips or sauces AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary and the user's observed choices remain conservative, including personal, right-TV, and front-social savory meals without dipping. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit or dessert-like food with a topping or dip, THEN less BECAUSE the personal strawberries-with-whipped-cream meal shows the user accepts some dipping in a quiet personal sweet-food context without escalating to maximal dipping. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal without sauce or dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals reinforce the conservative no-dip baseline when dipping is unnecessary.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations show a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when conversation or social attention may delay responses. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left or right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a very long wait while working in side-device contexts, reinforcing a stable preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a more manually controlled or complex meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE earlier front-working meals supported a very long intervention window when the user also chose confirmation and other explicit-control settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a straightforward savory meal with a more streamlined interaction style, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the front-laptop buffalo chicken meal showed the user may accept short waits in simpler working contexts when also skipping confirmation and favoring efficiency. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short wait in that easier front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that more complex front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode indicates an intermediate wait preference: more time than highly automated TV meals, but less than the very long waits used in work or socially distracting contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence shows the user may want a long intervention window there when choosing a more manually controlled interaction style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the new left-TV dessert-like meal shows that in the same environmental context the user can switch to a short wait when the overall interaction style is streamlined despite still using confirmation. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simpler sandwich-style savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the earlier observed meal reinforces a short wait preference in TV-viewing contexts where the user accepts more automation. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is more substantial savory food such as pizza with vegetables, THEN the user prefers a longer wait than in streamlined right-TV meals, with evidence spanning 100 sec to 1000 sec, BECAUSE right-TV savory meals show that wait time increases when the interaction becomes more manually controlled and cautious. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food with confirmation and other manual-control settings, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE repeated personal meals support an intermediate wait in quiet personal contexts when the user wants some intervention time but not the extremely long delays used in social or highly distracting settings. IF dining context evidence conflicts between preserving user intervention time and reducing delay AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is attended-target or manually controlled meals, THEN tie-break by desired automation level, favoring 10 sec when the user also chose a more streamlined flow, 100 sec for moderately manual but still efficient meals, and 1000 sec when the user wants strong protection against unwanted progression despite distraction or social demands BECAUSE the observed meals separate into these levels.
